E. Statement of facts.given_value: 58. 1. The latter application concerns the possible violations of the European Convention on Human Rights (‘ECHR/ Convention’) in regards to the Applicant's ('A.D.') husband's ('N.K.') arrest and the inability due to the Georgian legislation to appeal the legality of the arrest in the Courts, the lack of fair trial and the subsequent murder of N.K. during him being placed in custody of the law enforcement officers and the ineffectiveness of the investigation of his murder. Thus, the Applicant alleges the violations of both the negative and positive aspects of Article 2, Article 5, Article 6(1)(2) and Article 13. 2. On February 15, 2020, the applicant's husband - N.K., a well-known businessman, was arrested, as there was a suspicion that the latter was allegedly providing assistance to a terrorist organization operating in the occupied Tskhinvali region/the so-called South Ossetia (Annex (‘A’) 1). In 2018-2019, the mentioned organization allegedly carried out several terrorist attacks, including in the center of Tbilisi, which caused the deaths of civilians, as well as 35 police officers (A2). 3. On February 20, 2020, N.K. was charged with the offense of Article 323' of the Criminal Code of Georgia ("Illegal purchase, storage, carrying, manufacturing, transportation, sending, taking or use of firearms, ammunition, explosive substances or explosive devices for terrorist purposes for the purpose") under Section 5 ("the action provided for in Sections 1, 2, 3 or 4 of this Article, which caused the death of a person or other serious consequences") (A3; A15). 4. On the same day, the Tbilisi City Court used detention as a preventive measure (A4). The court shared the position of the prosecution that "the applicant, as a well-known businessman with ties to a terrorist organization operating outside the territory controlled by Georgia, had the opportunity to hide, obstruct the administration of justice and influence witnesses" (A4). On April 21, 2020, N.K. appealed against the decision of the Tbilisi City Court (AS), however, the Court of Appeals declared the appeal inadmissible on the basis of failure to meet procedural requirements (A6). 5. Considering the circumstances of the case and the personality of the Applicant’s husband, the case under consideration was actively covered by the media (A7). During the arrest of the Applicant’s husband, one of the ministers made a public statement (A8). In the mentioned statement, the minister, among other things, said that the law-enforcement agencies arrested one of the most dangerous criminals who provided assistance to the terrorist organization (A8). The said statement was widely disseminated by the media (A8.1). 6. The Tbilisi City Court found the applicant's husband guilty and sentenced him to 15 years of imprisonment (A9). The Court of Appeals shared the decision of the City Court (A10). The national courts considered that since the prosecution had limited access to the occupied territories, the indirect testimony of thirteen employees of the Applicant’s husband constituted sufficient evidence to prove the applicant's guilt (A9; A10). On February 1, 2022, the Supreme Court declared NK's application inadmissible (A11). 7. On February 2, 2022, during the transfer to the penitentiary institution, N.K. was murdered as a result of a bullet fired by an unidentified person (A12.1; A12.2.2). The fact of NK being transferred to a penitentiary institution was actively covered by the media before the aforementioned murder (A12.2.1). 8. On February 2, 2022, the General Prosecutor's Office of Georgia initiated a criminal investigation regarding this murder of the Applicant's husband (A12.1). The Applicant - A.D. was actively involved in the investigation (A13). However, the status of the victim has not been officially assigned, and until now, criminal prosecution against a specific person has not been initiated (A14).
E. Statement of facts.corrected_value: On February 15, 2020, the applicant's husband, N.K., was arrested on suspicion of providing assistance to a terrorist organization. He was charged with an offense under Article 323' of the Criminal Code of Georgia on February 20, 2020. The Tbilisi City Court used detention as a preventive measure on the same day. N.K. appealed against this decision on April 21, 2020, but the Court of Appeals declared the appeal inadmissible. The Tbilisi City Court found N.K. guilty and sentenced him to 15 years of imprisonment. The Court of Appeals upheld this decision. On February 1, 2022, the Supreme Court declared N.K.'s application inadmissible. N.K. was murdered on February 2, 2022, during his transfer to a penitentiary institution. The General Prosecutor's Office of Georgia initiated a criminal investigation into his murder on the same day.
E. Statement of facts.problem_in_given_value: The given value is too lengthy and includes unnecessary details. It also refers to annexes and other documents, which is not allowed according to the verification guidelines.
E. Statement of facts.verification_summary: The corrected value provides a concise and chronological account of the facts, as required by the verification guidelines. It includes the key events and decisions, such as the arrest, charge, detention, appeal, and murder of N.K., as well as the initiation of the criminal investigation.

Article 61.given_value: Convention. Explanation 1. Article 2 ranks as one of the most fundamental provisions in the Convention, one which in peace time, admits of no derogation under Article 15. Together with Article 3, it enshrines one of the basic values of the democratic societies making up the Council of Europe (Giuliani and Gaggio v. Italy [GC], no. 23458/02, 24.03.2011, § 174.). As such, its provisions must be strictly construed (McCann and Others v. the United Kingdom, no. 18984/91, 27.09.1995, § 147). Article 2 contains two substantive obligations: the
Article 61.problem_in_given_value: The given value does not provide a clear and concise statement of the alleged violation of Article 2, and it appears to be a quote from a legal text rather than a specific complaint.
Article 61.verification_summary: The admissibility guide requires a concise and legible statement of the alleged violation(s) and the relevant arguments. The given value does not meet this requirement.

Article 62.given_value: the Convention Explanation 5. Article 2 which imposes a duty on that State to secure the right to life by putting in place effective criminal-law provisions to deter the commission of offences against the person, backed up by law-enforcement machinery for the prevention, suppression and punishment of breaches of such provisions. This obligation requires by implication that there should be some form of effective official investigation when there is reason to believe that an individual has sustained life-threatening injuries in suspicious
Article 62.problem_in_given_value: The given value does not provide a clear and concise statement of the alleged violation of Article 2, and it appears to be a quote from a legal text rather than a specific complaint. It also lacks a specific explanation of how the Article was violated in relation to the facts in Part E.
Article 62.verification_summary: The application notes require a brief explanation of how the Article was violated, connected to the facts in Part E. The given value does not meet this requirement.

Complaints summary.problem_in_given_value: The field is blank, which may lead to inadmissibility due to the inability to verify the four-month time-limit.
Complaints summary.verification_summary: The complaints summary is required to assess admissibility and verify the exhaustion of domestic remedies.

Unused remedies.problem_in_given_value: None apparent, but requires completion of Field 65 to explain why a remedy was not used.
Unused remedies.verification_summary: The declaration of unused remedies is crucial for the Court to assess whether all effective domestic remedies have been exhausted.

Why unused.problem_in_given_value: The field is blank, which is inconsistent with the 'Yes' answer in the Unused remedies field and may lead to inadmissibility.
Why unused.verification_summary: An explanation is required to justify why a particular remedy was not used, especially if it was available and potentially effective.

Any proceedings.verification_summary: The field requires a Yes or No answer to determine if there are any parallel international proceedings

Details.verification_summary: This field should provide details of any parallel proceedings, including the name of the international body, dates, and outcomes

Other ECHR applications.verification_summary: The field requires a Yes or No answer to determine if there are any other ECHR applications related to the case

Application numbers.verification_summary: This field should include the application numbers of any previous ECHR applications, in the format XXXXX/YY

I. List of accompanying documents.problem_in_given_value: The list of accompanying documents is empty.
I. List of accompanying documents.verification_summary: The application requires a numbered, chronological list of all attached documents, including court decisions, appeals, medical reports, witness statements, and transcripts.

Additional comments.problem_in_given_value: Value is missing and cannot be inferred
Additional comments.verification_summary: Field is optional, but should only be used to flag something that does not fit elsewhere

Date.problem_in_given_value: Value is missing and is required for the declaration
Date.verification_summary: Date should be in the format DD/MM/YYYY and correspond to the date of dispatch

Signed by.problem_in_given_value: Value is missing and an original signature is required
Signed by.verification_summary: The declaration is a mandatory formal requirement and the form is not valid without an original signature

Correspondence contact.problem_in_given_value: Value is missing and is required for correspondence
Correspondence contact.verification_summary: The Court corresponds with only one applicant or one representative, and this field is required where there are multiple applicants or representatives
